summary: Split the old Kirby Zelda/Sheik copied neutral-special translation unit boundary into separate Zelda and Sheik source files, linked the Zelda slice as matching, and updated the build/split metadata accordingly. The PR moved Zelda-specific `ftKb_SpecialNZd` functions into a new `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c`, renamed the remaining old `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` file to `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, relocated shared/static data to the file owning its address range, and restored `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109260` to the Yoshi-copy file.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` containing the Zelda-copy neutral-special functions from `0x80105B2C` through `0x80105FEC`, including `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C`, `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105BA8`, `ftKb_ZdSpecialN_Anim`, `ftKb_ZdSpecialAirN_Anim`, IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks, and ground/air transition helpers.

Renamed `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialseak.c` and removed the Zelda-copy function block from it, leaving the later Sheik-copy range beginning at `fn_80105FEC`.

Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` ends at `0x80105B2C`, new `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` covers `.text 0x80105B2C-0x80105FEC` and `.sdata2 0x804D94F0-0x804D94F8`, and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` covers `.text 0x80105FEC-0x80109290` plus `.sdata2 0x804D94F8-0x804D9554`.
Updated `configure.py` to build `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)` and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)`, replacing the old nonmatching `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` entry.
Removed `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C` and two `ftKb_Init_804D94F0/804D94F4` externs from `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`, because the Zelda file now owns that start-of-Zelda code and its constants.
Moved `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109260` out of the renamed Sheik file and into `ftKb_SpecialNYs.c`, matching the split boundary where the Yoshi-copy special starts at `0x80109290`.

Moved or localized data definitions: `ftKb_Init_803CB770` became static in `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, `ftKb_Init_803B7570` and `ftKb_Init_803B757C` were defined in `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`, and their previous definitions/declarations were removed from `ftKb_Init.c` or old extern blocks.
smart_moves: Used address-range evidence from `splits.txt` to split one large `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` translation unit into Zelda and Sheik ownership instead of keeping unrelated copy abilities in one file.
Linked only the newly isolated Zelda slice as `Matching` while leaving the renamed Sheik slice `NonMatching`, preserving incremental progress without overclaiming the status of the remaining code.

Renamed files toward the lowercase `ftkirbyspecial<character>.c` convention already used by nearby Kirby copy-special files such as `ftkirbyspecialfox.c`, `ftkirbyspecialness.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`.
Kept constants and static arrays near the file whose split range owns them, reducing cross-file extern noise in `ftKb_Init.c` and the copied-special files.

Moved `ftKb_SpecialNYs_80109260` into `ftKb_SpecialNYs.c` rather than leaving a Yoshi-copy helper in the renamed Sheik file, making the source layout better reflect the actual symbol/range boundaries.
decomp_lessons: When a file named after one Kirby copied special spans multiple copied characters, use the linker split ranges to identify natural file boundaries and create per-character translation units.
A successful split can turn a small address range into a matching object while leaving adjacent unresolved code nonmatching; decomp progress does not require solving the whole old file at once.
After splitting, audit adjacent files for functions that were temporarily placed across boundaries, such as a function at the start of the next module or helper constants stored in an init file.
Build configuration and split metadata must be updated together: `configure.py` object entries should mirror `config/GALE01/splits.txt` source names and matching status.
Moving data definitions can be part of matching/link cleanup: remove obsolete externs and define arrays/constants in the file that owns the relevant data section range.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special source files use lowercase `ftkirbyspecial<copied_character>.c` naming in this area, e.g. `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` and `ftkirbyspecialseak.c`.
The PR uses `seak` in the filename, not `sheik`, matching the repository's existing or intended spelling for this Kirby copy-special module.

Function names in the moved code still retain address-style `ftKb_SpecialNZd_*` and `ftKb_ZdSpecial*` symbols; the PR primarily renames/splits files rather than fully semantically renaming every function.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Isolated the exact matching Zelda range `0x80105B2C-0x80105FEC` as its own object and marked only that object `Matching` in `configure.py`.

Preserved stack-padding and matching-sensitive idioms in the moved Zelda code, including `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(20)`, the `volatile unsigned int pad`, `void* new_var` permuter slop, and the `fp->cmd_vars[0] == ((0, 0))` expression.

Replaced `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` arguments that previously referenced external `ftKb_Init_804D94F0/804D94F4` with literal `0.0f` and `1.0f` in the new Zelda file, consistent with owning the corresponding `.sdata2` range.
Adjusted `.sdata2` ownership so Zelda receives `0x804D94F0-0x804D94F8` and Sheik receives `0x804D94F8-0x804D9554`, avoiding data-section overlap after the split.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mark `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialseak.c` as matching.
Search for remaining `ftKb_SpecialNZd` symbols to see whether function names were later semantically renamed for Zelda-copy neutral special.
Check whether `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` spelling was retained or later renamed to `ftkirbyspecialsheik.c`.
Audit adjacent Kirby copied-special files for more functions placed across split boundaries.
